blocks.content: We walked back. Halfway home Jonathan went quiet in the specific way he goes quiet before he says something he's been building up to.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: How are you doing. For real.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm not saying jump into something. I'm saying — she was asking Keisha about you this week. She noticed you were off.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't need to talk to Maya right now.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know you don't need to. I'm saying it might help. Crystal is gone. Maya is here. That's just facts.
Crystal is gone. Maya is here. The way he said it — like those were two clean categories you could just sort between. Like the first one being gone made the second one a natural next step.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know what you're trying to do. Goodnight.
He let it go. For that night.
I walked the last two blocks alone. The phone was quiet in my pocket.
